perturbation.original_text: All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.changed_text: All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party, except when disclosed to third-party consultants; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'except when disclosed to third-party consultants' in Section 3(a), we create an in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly states that the Evaluation Material 'shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party.' The addition allows for disclosure to third parties, contradicting the initial confidentiality requirement. This creates uncertainty about the scope of the confidentiality obligations and whether third-party consultants are exempt from it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality of Evaluation Material. All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party, except when disclosed to third-party consultants; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Evaluation Material 'shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party'. The changed text adds 'except when disclosed to third-party consultants,' which directly contradicts the initial confidentiality requirement. The contradicted text is an exact repetition of the changed text and thus, the introduction of the exception undeniably creates an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives, except that the Receiving Party can keep a copy of the Derivative Evaluation Material to use in future, unrelated transactions..
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'except that the Receiving Party can keep a copy of the Derivative Evaluation Material to use in future, unrelated transactions' in Section 6, we are creating an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the Receiving Party 'destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material'. The addition allows the Receiving Party to retain a copy of the derivative Evaluation Material. This contradicts the initial obligation to destroy all derivative evaluation material, creating uncertainty and allowing for potential misuse of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return and Destruction of Evaluation Material. At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives, except that the Receiving Party can keep a copy of the Derivative Evaluation Material to use in future, unrelated transactions.. Nothing herein shall obligate the Receiving Party to provide any Derivative Evaluation Material to the Furnishing Party. Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party 'destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material'. The changed text adds an exception allowing the Receiving Party to retain a copy of the derivative Evaluation Material to use in future, unrelated transactions. This directly contradicts the initial obligation to destroy all derivative evaluation material, creating a clear inconsistency and in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and changed text are in the same location, but the addition creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For a period one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Party shall, without the prior written consent of the other Party or the other Party's board of directors, either directly or indirectly through any affiliate or Representative or otherwise: (i) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, tender offer, merger, consolidation, share exchange or otherwise, ownership or control of any voting securities, or any direct or indirect right to acquire any voting securities, of the other Party, any subsidiary thereof, or any successor corporation thereto; (ii) make, or in any way participate in any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the rules and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the other Party; (iii) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the other Party; (iv) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for, or offer of any merger, acquisition or other business combination or extraordinary transaction involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries or any securities or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries; (v) form, join or in any way participate in a "group"( as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing, or (vi) request the other Party or any of the other party's Representatives to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph 5(b) in any manner which may reasonably be expected to compel or result in public disclosure; provided that such limitations shall expire upon the public announcement of a third party tender offer for more than fifty percent of the outstanding voting 3 securities of the other Party or a business combination between the other Party and a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Party shall, without the prior written consent of the other Party or the other Party's board of directors, either directly or indirectly through any affiliate or Representative or otherwise: (i) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, tender offer, merger, consolidation, share exchange or otherwise, ownership or control of any voting securities, or any direct or indirect right to acquire any voting securities, of the other Party, any subsidiary thereof, or any successor corporation thereto; (ii) make, or in any way participate in any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the rules and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the other Party; (iii) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the other Party; (iv) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for, or offer of any merger, acquisition or other business combination or extraordinary transaction involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries or any securities or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries; (v) form, join or in any way participate in a "group"( as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing, or (vi) request the other Party or any of the other party's Representatives to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph 5(b) in any manner which may reasonably be expected to compel or result in public disclosure; provided that such limitations shall expire upon the public announcement of a third party tender offer for more than fifty percent of the outstanding voting 3 securities of the other Party or a business combination between the other Party and a third party, unless the other party has previously breached this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase ', unless the other party has previously breached this agreement.' to Section 5(b), we introduce a contradiction. The original text outlines specific limitations on actions either party can take regarding acquiring or influencing the other party, specifying that these limitations expire only upon a third-party tender offer or business combination. However, the add-on introduces a condition that the limitations can also expire if the other party has breached the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces an exception not previously mentioned. Now, the expiration of these limitations is based on two conditions: a third-party action OR the other party breaching the agreement. Thus making it contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period one year from the date of this Agreement, neither Party shall, without the prior written consent of the other Party or the other Party's board of directors, either directly or indirectly through any affiliate or Representative or otherwise: (i) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, tender offer, merger, consolidation, share exchange or otherwise, ownership or control of any voting securities, or any direct or indirect right to acquire any voting securities, of the other Party, any subsidiary thereof, or any successor corporation thereto; (ii) make, or in any way participate in any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the rules and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the other Party; (iii) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the other Party; (iv) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for, or offer of any merger, acquisition or other business combination or extraordinary transaction involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries or any securities or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries; (v) form, join or in any way participate in a "group"( as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing, or (vi) request the other Party or any of the other party's Representatives to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph 5(b) in any manner which may reasonably be expected to compel or result in public disclosure; provided that such limitations shall expire upon the public announcement of a third party tender offer for more than fifty percent of the outstanding voting 3 securities of the other Party or a business combination between the other Party and a third party, unless the other party has previously breached this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which the limitations in Section 5(b) expire. The original text states that the limitations expire only upon a third-party tender offer or business combination. The changed text adds an additional condition: the limitations also expire if the other party has breached the agreement. This creates a direct conflict, as the expiration is no longer solely dependent on the third-party actions, thereby introducing an in-text contradiction within the document following the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type definition.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder, except obligations related to derivative material.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase ', except obligations related to derivative material' in Section 6, we introduce a contradiction to the general confidentiality obligations outlined in the contract. The original text states that the Receiving Party remains bound by all confidentiality obligations even after returning or destroying the Evaluation Material. The addition now provides an exception, removing the confidentiality obligations specifically related to derivative material. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding what information remains confidential after termination of discussions and after return, distruction or erasure, and opens a door to potential disputes over the use of derivative information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return and Destruction of Evaluation Material. At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives, except that the Receiving Party can keep a copy of the Derivative Evaluation Material to use in future, unrelated transactions.. Nothing herein shall obligate the Receiving Party to provide any Derivative Evaluation Material to the Furnishing Party. Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder, except obligations related to derivative material.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Receiving Party remains bound by all confidentiality obligations even after returning or destroying the Evaluation Material. The changed text adds an exception, removing the confidentiality obligations specifically related to derivative material. The contradicted text includes the changed text and shows that the Receiving Party is allowed to keep a copy of the derivative evaluation material to use in the future. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding what information remains confidential after termination of discussions and after return, distruction or erasure, regarding the derivative material, as per the explanation. This aligns with 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the placement of the clause creates conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the confidentiality of their discussions, negotiations and investigations concerning a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the confidentiality of their discussions, negotiations and investigations concerning a Transaction, however, verbal agreements made prior to this agreement will also be in effect.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase ', however, verbal agreements made prior to this agreement will also be in effect.', we are creating an in-text contradiction in Section 8(b). The original text asserts that the written agreement is the *sole and entire* agreement. Adding this phrase creates a contradictory and ambiguous arrangement that contradicts the sole and entire agreement by attempting to incorporate earlier verbal understandings, as those verbal agreements could contradict the writting on this current agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement. This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the confidentiality of their discussions, negotiations and investigations concerning a Transaction, however, verbal agreements made prior to this agreement will also be in effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement contains the *sole and entire* agreement between the parties. The changed text adds that verbal agreements made prior to this agreement will also be in effect. This directly contradicts the 'sole and entire' clause, as it introduces the possibility that other agreements (the verbal ones) are also part of the agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the explanation clearly states how this change causes a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party consents and submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts in the State of Georgia, and the city of Atlanta, for the adjudication of any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or otherwise relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party consents and submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts in the State of Georgia, and the city of Atlanta, for the adjudication of any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or otherwise relating to this Agreement, unless the Furnishing Party prefers arbitration in New York.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase ', unless the Furnishing Party prefers arbitration in New York.' to Section 8(f), we create an in-text contradiction. The original text states that jurisdiction is *exclusively* in Georgia courts. The addition allows the Furnishing Party to choose arbitration in New York. This directly contradicts the original jurisdiction clause, since the addition can disregard the georgia courts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law; Forum. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the internal laws of the State of Georgia, without regard to the principles of the conflict of laws thereof. Each Party consents and submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts in the State of Georgia, and the city of Atlanta, for the adjudication of any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or otherwise relating to this Agreement, unless the Furnishing Party prefers arbitration in New York.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction in Georgia courts. The changed text introduces an exception allowing the Furnishing Party to choose arbitration in New York, which directly contradicts the original clause's exclusivity. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which reinforces the presence of a direct contradiction introduced by the modification. The explanation clearly states that the addition allows a party to disregard the Georgia courts, creating the contradiction.
